spec_match.rationale: Demo invokes the relevant API surface (WebGPU: 3D texture support for BC and ASTC compressed formats) and surfaces the result in the page, demonstrating the spec behaviour the feature ships.

interactive.rationale: Real interactive controls drive the page (0 buttons, 3 inputs, 1 selects, 3 ranges, 1 listeners) — well past the static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept page lede restates and delivers on the framing used in the feature index card.

edge_cases.rationale: Only the happy path is demonstrated — no visible error / unsupported / boundary handling for the user to explore.

references.rationale: Page does not cite the required chromestatus.com/feature/5080855386783744 link — breaks the slug self-heal.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex of note, and button overrides keep background + color paired (CLAUDE.md rules 5 and 6).

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate failure and edge-case branches
openQuestions.detail: Add visible branches for the unsupported / unavailable / error / boundary inputs paths. For built-in AI APIs, that means 'downloadable', 'downloading', 'unavailable' availability states; for HTTP features, error responses and CSP blocks; for CSS features, zero / extreme / inherited values. Today only the happy path is reachable from the UI.

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus.com/feature link
openQuestions.detail: CLAUDE.md rule 3 requires every page to embed https://chromestatus.com/feature/5080855386783744. The self-heal slug-fix script reads this link to recover from misslugged folders. Add it under a 'see also' section.

summary: 'Volume Texture Budget Designer' misses a non-trivial part of the rubric (2 fail / 0 partial). The open questions list captures the highest-leverage fixes — usually adding a real interactive control or wiring the chromestatus reference link.
